question: Explain the operating principle of the DHRS (Diverse Heat Removal System).

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components in the NuScale reactor, specifying alloys and standards for each part to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It references relevant sections of the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and lists the specific alloys and material specifications for the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, heater sheaths, and bolting.

nuscale_ch05_0022.title: Decay Heat Removal System
nuscale_ch05_0022.summary: The Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) is an engineered safety feature designed to remove residual and core decay heat during non-LOCA events when normal cooling is unavailable, ensuring safe shutdown of the NuScale Power Module without external power. The system maintains reactor coolant system (RCS) temperatures below 420°F within 36 hours, protecting fuel and pressure boundaries, and its reliability and risk significance are evaluated through established programs and criteria.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section outlines the testing and inspection requirements for the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) components in the NuScale reactor, ensuring compliance with ASME codes for Class 2 components. It specifies that DHRS actuation valves are exercised during cold shutdown conditions, and that various functional and position verification tests are performed according to technical specifications, including a one-time in-situ test for the first reactor module during initial startup.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear remaining the most likely degradation mechanism and corrosion risks assessed as low.

edges.description: Section A describes the materials and components of the pressurizer, which is part of the Reactor Coolant System, while Section B explains the design basis and function of the DHRS, which relies on the Reactor Coolant System to remove decay heat; thus, Section A provides component details that support the system-level function described in Section B.
Section A specifies inspection and classification requirements for DHRS components, which are part of the system whose operating principle and safety function are described in Section B.

Section B provides references to material descriptions and component details that are relevant to the reactor coolant system piping discussed in Section A, establishing a structural link between the two sections.

Section B explains the function and design basis of the DHRS, which relies on the Reactor Coolant System described in Section A as its heat removal pathway, connecting the physical system (A) to the operational principle (B).

Section A specifies inspection and classification requirements for DHRS components, including steam piping and valves, which are part of the reactor coolant system piping discussed in Section B, thus Section A provides details for the general system described in Section B.
